title: Repliclaw Result Envelope
description: Standard outer envelope every task skill must emit via <<RESULT>>. The `data` field is task-owned and validated separately against the task's own schema.

status.description: Outcome class. ok=fully succeeded. error=hard failure. partial=some actions succeeded, some failed. timeout=ran out of time. declined=replica refused (policy/safety). needs-input=did everything possible, blocked on a human/parent decision.

taskName.description: Pulled from task SKILL.md frontmatter `name`. Echoed for audit replay.

taskVersion.description: Pulled from task SKILL.md frontmatter `version`. Lets orchestrators reason about contract evolution.

runId.description: Unique ID for this run. Matches audit log filename.

inputs.description: Echo of inputs passed to the task. For reproducibility.

actions.description: Every external side-effect the replica took. The audit gold.

notes.description: Observations the replica wants to flag for the parent/orchestrator that aren't side-effects. Machine-readable hints.

errors.description: Structured errors. Empty array for status=ok.

data.description: Task-owned freeform payload. Validated against the task's own outputs_schema ONLY when status is 'ok' or 'partial'. Error-ish statuses (error / timeout / declined / needs-input) skip data validation — their data is empty/minimal by design since the task didn't complete normally. Invariants (task-declared cross-field checks) run under the same gate.

type.description: Action type in `<service>.<resource>.<verb>` form (e.g. jira.comment.create, filemage.user.create, gmail.message.send, op.item.create).

ref.description: External ID or reference for the resource touched (e.g. SS-1234, msg-id, user-id). Optional for actions without a meaningful ref.

details.description: Action-type-specific payload. Required for any mutating action (anything other than read/get/list).

status.description: success=replica performed the action. failed=replica tried and failed (details should explain). skipped=deliberately not performed (e.g. dry-run, idempotency guard hit). planned=replica is declaring intent but did NOT execute; the parent is expected to execute (used for parent-executed action types like slack.message.send).

payload.description: For status=planned only. The content the parent needs to execute the action (e.g. message body, email HTML, file bytes-ref). Kept separate from `details` so audit-replay stays compact: `details` is the addressable shape (channel, recipient, subject), `payload` is the thing being shipped.

then.description: planned actions must carry enough info for the parent to execute.

code.description: Stable error code (e.g. filemage.api.4xx, gmail.send.quota, jira.transition.invalid).
